items.title: Randall says concussion left her 'no choice' but to retire from AFLW

children.content: Adelaide Crows star Chelsea Randall announces her retirement from the AFLW, citing concussion as a major factor in her decision to hang up her boots and step away from the game.

gallery.altText: Adelaide Crows AFLW star Chelsea Randall.

thumbnail.altText: Adelaide Crows AFLW star Chelsea Randall.

items.title: Power tools used in 'stupid' and potentially lethal acts of Stobie pole vandalism

children.content: Members of the public were put at "severe risk" of electrocution after four Stobie poles were vandalised in Adelaide's north in the past month, causing some to fall and expose high voltage electrical lines.

image.altText: A damaged Stobie pole lies on the ground near a popular walking and mountain bike track in the Cobbler Creek Recreation Park

items.title: Four fatal crashes in 12 days rock SA's Limestone Coast

children.content: Police are calling on drivers to stay attentive and slow down after four people were killed on one region's roads in just 12 days.

image.altText: A bunch of flowers placed next to a road.

items.title: Network 10 strikes last-minute deal to extend broadcasting in SA and NSW

children.content: Two regions in South Australia and one in New South Wales were set to lose access to free-to-air Network 10 services in coming days, but a new agreement means coverage will continue until the end of September.

image.altText: Sign of the number 10 in a blue circle on a tower with trees visible

items.title: Family who spent $250,000 on IVF welcomes new rebate scheme in SA

children.content: A South Australian couple who spent $250,000 on fertility treatments say the government's IVF rebate will make a "huge difference" for people trying to conceive.

image.altText: A man holds a toddler while a woman speaks into media microphones outside a house

items.title: Ageing 'Nashos' call for priority as all veterans get equal access to health benefits

children.content: All Australian veterans will soon have access to claim compensation for health issues, fulfilling a royal commission recommendation, but those conscripted during the Vietnam War say their advanced age means their claims should receive priority.

gallery.altText: An old photo of young soldiers in a casual setting
A man with a bald head wearing a checked shirt in front of a window
War medals in cases on a table

thumbnail.altText: An old photo of young soldiers in a casual setting
A man with a bald head wearing a checked shirt in front of a window
War medals in cases on a table

items.title: Thousands of outback roads damaged by floods could take year to fix

children.content: Damage to South Australia's network of outback roads is isolating remote communities, preventing children from getting to school, and slowing the restocking process for pastoralists recovering from drought.

image.altText: A man holding a tape measure down the edge of a washed out dirt road

items.title: Housing shortage does not deter town from embracing migrants

children.content: The township of Naracoorte, nestled in South Australia's rural heartland, has put out the welcome mat for migrants, who have helped enrich its social fabric and boost the economy.

image.altText: A man stands in a grocery store smiling, holding a box of baby toys.

items.title: The dry heart of Australia is now awash with water

children.content: The myth of an inland sea lured 19th century European explorers into the heart of Australia. But where they found only desert, this year it's awash, with water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre now the best some locals have ever seen.

articles.title: Heavy rain to soak Australia's east as un-El-Niño-like weather continues

children.content: For an El Niño year, 2026 is so far the wettest on record for Australia, and an influx of tropical moisture is about to deliver another round of widespread rain.

image.altText: a rainbow and heavy dark clouds over the water at port macquarie

gallery.altText: birds eye view of a bright pink lake, with a large body of blue water in the background
Hundreds of pelicans sitting down on flat, brown ground
An aerial photo of Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre

thumbnail.altText: birds eye view of a bright pink lake, with a large body of blue water in the background
Hundreds of pelicans sitting down on flat, brown ground
An aerial photo of Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre

items.title: 'Worst' wind lull pushes green poster child South Australia to its power limits

children.content: South Australia is at the bleeding edge of the energy transition but record low winds have pushed its grid to the brink of its limits.

image.altText: Two wind turbines stand side by side against a hazy blue sky

items.title: Volunteers say hotline told them to collect dead bird against government advice

children.content: South Australian citizen scientists say they called the Emergency Animal Disease Hotline to report a dead sea bird and were given advice that contradicts official government messaging.

image.altText: A dead pied cormorant in shallow beach water

items.title: Vanuatu critical infrastructure to remain 'free from militarisation' under pact

children.content: Vanuatu's Prime Minister Jotham Napat and Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese ink the Nakamal Agreement, almost 10 months after Mr Napat pulled out of a planned signing ceremony in Port Vila, citing concerns around sovereignty.

image.altText: Jotham Napat and Anthony Albanese hold up signed documents.

items.title: 'Terrible tragedy': Minister won't request coronial inquest into baby's death

children.content: WA's health minister says she would support a coronial inquest into the death of an eight-month-old girl at Kalgoorlie Health Campus, but says it is a decision for the state coroner.

image.altText: A baby sitting in hospital bed looking at camera

items.title: Bronze tugboat propellers worth $2m stolen in WA's north

children.content: In a heist not seen before in this WA mining town, thieves have allegedly made away with eight tonnes of tugboat propellers, with the copper estimated to be worth an eye-watering $2 million.

gallery.altText: Bronze propellers in yellow frames in an industrial yard.

thumbnail.altText: Bronze propellers in yellow frames in an industrial yard.

items.title: Court finds staffer's relationship with 20yo parolee 'grossly inappropriate'

children.content: An Alice Springs parole worker found in a hotel room with the 20-year-old she was responsible for supporting has avoided prison for attempting to pervert the course of justice.

image.altText: A woman outside the court hides her face behind a cardigan, supporters around her attempt to block the view with umbrellas.

items.title: Woman feared 'being sent to hell' if she escaped cult, court told

children.content: An alleged victim of cult leader William "Little Pebble" Kamm has told a court she began experiencing extreme anxiety as she pondered an escape from a property on the NSW South Coast.

image.altText: small grey-haired man in chinos and jumper crossing street surrounded by men in suits with suitcases

items.title: Jillaroos, jackaroos and an iconic event: The Brunette Races in pictures

children.content: More than 800 people from across northern Australia hit a remote outback cattle station over the weekend for the iconic annual Brunette Races.

gallery.altText: A cowboy on top of a brown horse against a black night sky mid air as he is bucked from his horse.
High-angle shot of cowboy holding a saddle on grassy grounds, yellow.
A man in a large black cowboy hat pulls a saddle apart

thumbnail.altText: A cowboy on top of a brown horse against a black night sky mid air as he is bucked from his horse.
High-angle shot of cowboy holding a saddle on grassy grounds, yellow.
A man in a large black cowboy hat pulls a saddle apart

items.title: ICAC hears uni executive sacked the day he rejected colleague's pay rise

children.content: The ICAC inquiry has heard a former University of Wollongong executive was dismissed on the day of a disputed salary increase.

image.altText: Sign in glass wall saying welcome to UOW

items.title: Alleged Russian spies charged with new espionage offence

children.content: Crown prosecutors presented a new bench charge for the couple of conspiracy to commit espionage.

image.altText: A composite image of the woman and man in seperate images.

items.title: Lauryn Hill credited with reshaping music and inspiring generations

children.content: American musician and actress Lauryn Hill has been recognised with the inaugural Living Legend Icon Award at the BET Awards by the Black Entertainment Television network in the United States.

gallery.altText: Lauryn Hill holding a mic and singing on stage in a ruffly gown, short curly hair

thumbnail.altText: Lauryn Hill holding a mic and singing on stage in a ruffly gown, short curly hair

items.title: Four ways to turn tinned chickpeas into a delicious dinner

children.content: From comforting curry to hearty soup, these easy recipes will get chickpeas out of the pantry and onto the menu.

image.altText: A bowl of pasta, chickpea ad spinach soup with a napkin and crusty bread nearby, a heart family meal.

items.title: Outback town set to star in new thriller as film festival kicks off

children.content: Red Dog director Kriv Stenders reveals plans for a new thriller film as the annual Vision Splendid Outback Film Festival kicks off in Winton.

image.altText: two men at the gala opening of the winton film festival

items.title: NASA to bump telescope back into space to avoid it falling to Earth

children.content: US space agency NASA is racing to save an aging telescope, used to observe cosmic explosions, from falling back to Earth with a daring rescue mission.

gallery.altText: A digital graphic illustration of a copper yellow telescope vehicle with light grey panel wings, seen on a galaxy backdrop.

thumbnail.altText: A digital graphic illustration of a copper yellow telescope vehicle with light grey panel wings, seen on a galaxy backdrop.

items.title: Ben Stokes's retirement a perfect example of his main character energy

children.content: Ben Stokes has been the centre of England's cricketing universe for 15 years. In announcing his retirement mid-match, he highlighted how damaging that main character energy can be.

image.altText: Ben Stokes rubs his face

items.title: Surgeon sounded 'exhausted' a week before fatal crash, brother says

children.content: The brother of Royal Hobart Hospital doctor Artyom Avetisyan says the cardiothoracic surgery registrar sounded "extremely exhausted" in the week before his crash death.

image.altText: Artyom Avetisyan wears a suit and tie and holds a stethoscope while standing in front of a wooden wall.

items.title: Les Mills, NZ Olympian and founder of global fitness brand, dies aged 91

children.content: The New Zealand Olympian who opened an Auckland gym in 1968 that grew into an international group fitness brand has died aged 91.

gallery.altText: A man throwing a shot put.

thumbnail.altText: A man throwing a shot put.

items.title: Bruce Lehrmann loses legal bid for Brittany Higgins documentary footage

children.content: Former Liberal staffer Bruce Lehrmann has been knocked back in a bid to gain access to raw documentary footage which featured Brittany Higgins.

image.altText: A man in a suit and tie

items.title: Allan government under pressure amid latest Big Build corruption allegations

children.content: The Victorian premier has backed her government's response to corruption in the construction industry, despite criticism from across politics and integrity experts.

image.altText: A black sign with 'CFMEU Victoria' written on it, hanging on a construction site.

items.title: France records 1,000 excess daily deaths as record heatwave broken by severe storms

children.content: More than 150 million Europeans swelter in "extreme heat", which scientists say has been the worst the continent has ever recorded.

image.altText: Lightning striking behind the Eiffel Tower at night with many onlookers.

gallery.altText: Men, women and children bathing in fountain water in front of the Eiffel Tower.

thumbnail.altText: Men, women and children bathing in fountain water in front of the Eiffel Tower.
